mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real personal consumption expenditures, proxied by the retail sales series RSAFS, signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 20-day SPX returns below 0.05, or average forward return in top quintile of RSAFS growth is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.0%, it signals a weak labor market and recessionary conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: After UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, average 60-day SPX return is positive or equal to baseline (mean return during non-trigger periods) at significance level 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising federal funds rate and declining industrial production indicate that monetary tightening is choking real economic activity, a classic late-cycle signal that depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase and INDPRO month-over-month decrease
failure_condition: In months where both conditions hold, average 20-day forward SPX return is positive or not significantly different from overall average (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production troughs, identified by a local minimum in INDPRO, mark the end of a supply-side contraction and the beginning of a recovery phase, during which equity markets rally strongly over the subsequent week (5 trading days) as output rebounds.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO reaches a local minimum (lower than previous 6 months)
failure_condition: Following INDPRO troughs, 5-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of cases, or average return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal accelerating labor market weakness that erodes household income and confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month (20 trading days).
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases for two consecutive months
failure_condition: After two consecutive PAYEMS declines, average 20-day SPX forward return is positive or not less than -0.5% (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion of real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad economic growth that supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days), regardless of other market conditions.
failure_condition: Rolling 5-year Rank IC between GDPC1 quarterly growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns below 0.04, or top-quintile GDP growth periods produce negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2% while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, real interest rates decline, leading to an overheating economy and eventual monetary tightening that hurts equities; negative S&P 500 returns are expected over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 2% and FEDFUNDS unchanged or lower than previous month
failure_condition: In triggered months, average 20-day SPX forward return is positive or not significantly different from the overall average (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 5% signals surging construction activity and economic momentum, which quickly translates into positive equity market sentiment and gains over the next week (5 trading days).
failure_condition: After a >5% monthly HOUST increase, 5-day SPX forward return is negative in more than 50% of cases, or average return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
